current_approach_summary: The generator is implementing a traditional Rust cache with separate error types for each subsystem (concurrency, TTL, memory) but lacks a unified error handling strategy that maintains consistency across all subsystems during error recovery.

alternatives.strategy: Implement a centralized error coordinator pattern with a unified error state machine that tracks cross-subsystem dependencies and orchestrates recovery procedures. Create a single CacheError enum that encapsulates all subsystem errors with recovery metadata, and use a coordinator component that ensures atomic rollback across all affected subsystems when any error occurs.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: This addresses the measurement spec by ensuring error recovery maintains cache-wide consistency rather than leaving subsystems in inconsistent states through coordinated rollback procedures.
alternatives.example_signal: A CacheErrorCoordinator that tracks which subsystems are affected by each operation and can rollback TTL updates, memory allocations, and lock states atomically when any subsystem fails.

alternatives.strategy: Design a transactional error handling system using a two-phase commit pattern where each subsystem (concurrency, TTL, memory, async) implements prepare/commit/abort phases. All operations go through a transaction manager that ensures either all subsystems succeed or all are rolled back to their previous consistent state.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: This ensures error handling strategies are consistent across all subsystems by treating cache operations as distributed transactions that maintain ACID properties.
alternatives.example_signal: Each cache operation returns a Transaction<T> that can be committed only after all subsystems have successfully prepared, with automatic rollback if any subsystem fails during prepare or commit phases.

alternatives.strategy: Implement a hierarchical error context system where errors bubble up through subsystem layers with accumulated context, and use a global error handler that can reconstruct the full system state from error context to perform intelligent recovery. Each subsystem contributes recovery hints to a shared error context that guides system-wide recovery decisions.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: This creates consistent error handling by ensuring all subsystems contribute to a unified error context that enables cache-wide consistency during recovery operations.
alternatives.example_signal: An ErrorContext that accumulates subsystem state snapshots and recovery procedures, allowing the global handler to restore all subsystems to a consistent state based on the accumulated context.

recommendation: Alternative 1 (centralized error coordinator) because it directly addresses the core issue of maintaining cache-wide consistency during error recovery while being simpler to implement than distributed transactions.
